evidence_label: mitapivat evidence-to-action map, not medical advice

evidence_states.source_type: FDA label and official product site
evidence_states.public_fact: AQVESME is indicated for anemia in adults with alpha- or beta-thalassemia in the United States.
evidence_states.allowed_action: Use as an adult-label benchmark and source for clinician questions.
evidence_states.blocked_action: Do not infer pediatric safety, pediatric effectiveness, local access, or case relevance.

evidence_states.source_type: ClinicalTrials.gov NCT04770779 and official product site
evidence_states.public_fact: The adult TDT ENERGIZE-T registry snapshot is active-not-recruiting with posted results; the official product site reports transfusion-reduction response of 30.4 percent for AQVESME versus 12.6 percent for placebo.
evidence_states.allowed_action: Use as an adult TDT transfusion-reduction benchmark.
evidence_states.blocked_action: Do not treat adult TDT results as pediatric, local-access, or individual eligibility evidence.

evidence_states.public_fact: The pediatric TDT mitapivat phase 3 record is not yet recruiting, has no posted results, and uses transfusion-reduction response through week 48 as the primary outcome.
evidence_states.allowed_action: Track as a pediatric TDT registry watchlist and clinician-mediated trial-contact question.
evidence_states.blocked_action: Do not cite as pediatric efficacy, access, or case eligibility evidence.

evidence_states.public_fact: The pediatric NTDT mitapivat phase 3 record is not yet recruiting, has no posted results, and uses hemoglobin response as the primary outcome.
evidence_states.allowed_action: Track as a pediatric NTDT watchlist only.
evidence_states.blocked_action: Do not mix this record with transfusion-dependent case questions.

evidence_states.source_type: FDA label and official product site
evidence_states.public_fact: The FDA label and official product site describe liver-injury risk, REMS constraints, and drug-interaction review needs.
evidence_states.allowed_action: Route safety and monitoring questions to qualified clinicians and proper product owners.
evidence_states.blocked_action: Do not publish dosing, monitoring instructions, adverse-event narratives, or patient-specific advice.

evidence_states.public_fact: Case relevance remains blocked until qualified clinician review of diagnosis, age and weight band, transfusion burden, iron and organ status, liver tests, medication list, and local access constraints.
evidence_states.allowed_action: Ask a clinician whether mitapivat is worth reviewing after the minimum packet is complete enough.
evidence_states.blocked_action: Do not recommend mitapivat, infer eligibility, or change transfusion or chelation care.

decision_rule: Promote adult mitapivat evidence only as a benchmark. Keep pediatric records as watchlist evidence until recruitment/results or clinician-mediated owner confirmation changes the state. Keep case-001 relevance blocked until clinician review.
